title: Verify Quiz Pregenerate Test
content:
Introduction to Async Testing

Async testing verifies that background jobs report progress correctly and
produce a usable result once complete. A good async job design writes
progress checkpoints so a polling client can show a meaningful status
message instead of a generic spinner.

Key Concepts of Job Polling

A job has a job_id, a status (pending, processing, done, error), and a
stage describing the current step. Clients poll a status endpoint on an
interval until the job reaches a terminal status. This pattern avoids
blocking the HTTP request for long-running work like calling an LLM
multiple times to generate quiz questions.

lessons.title: Introduction to Async Testing
lessons.content: This section serves as an entry point to the course, introducing learners to the fundamental concept of asynchronous testing. It establishes the groundwork for understanding how to evaluate and verify background operations.

lessons.title: Async testing verifies that background jobs report progress...
lessons.content: Asynchronous testing ensures that background processes successfully communicate their status and deliver a viable end product. By designing jobs to log progress checkpoints, client systems can retrieve and display detailed status updates instead of keeping users waiting with a generic loading graphic.

lessons.title: Key Concepts of Job Polling
lessons.content: This section focuses on the primary concepts surrounding job polling. It acts as an overview of how systems repeatedly check the progress of asynchronous tasks.

lessons.title: A job has a job_id, a status (pending, processing, done, err...
lessons.content: Asynchronous jobs are tracked using a unique identifier, an explicit stage indicator, and a state flow that moves from pending and processing to terminal states like done or error. Clients query a dedicated status endpoint at regular intervals to check this state, which prevents long-running operations—such as calling an LLM to generate quiz questions—from locking up HTTP connections.
